_note: ROUND 3 (great-grandparents) for batch d_800_1400_4 (medieval 800-1400 AD): documented parents (+ selected notable full siblings) for the 34 round-2 grandparent ids in that batch, going ONE generation further back. NEW nodes use the R3E_ prefix. 31 of 34 filled; 3 SKIPPED as insufficiently documented (no fabrication): (a) R2I_MARGUERITE_AULNAY (Marguerite d'Aulnay, wife of Bertrand III des Baux) — her parents (Robert d'Aulnay + Isabella Stendardo) appear only in user-generated genealogy databases (Geni/WikiTree/MyHeritage), no Wikipedia or scholarly source; (b) R2H_FERNANGUTIERREZHINESTROSA (Fernán Gutiérrez de Hinestrosa) — his own parents are named only in an 18th-c. genealogy (Juan de Ariza, 1772) which gives TWO possible mothers, no reliable primary/modern source (his wife Estefanía and children are documented, but not his ascendancy); (c) R2H_MALCOLMDRUMMOND10 (Sir Malcolm Drummond, '10th Thane of Lennox') — the Drummond 'Thanes of Lennox' pedigree is a discredited later construct; sources are genealogy databases only (Geni/thePeerage) and the mother is unnamed ('a daughter of Graham'). MAJOR DEDUP / CROSS-LINKS to EXISTING nodes (grepped before writing): (1) R2F_JAMESII_ARAGON (James II of Aragon) ALREADY EXISTS (created in 41_grandparents_d3.json as a child of R2F_PETERIII_ARAGON + R2F_CONSTANCE_SICILY_ARAGON) — referenced as father of Alfonso IV, NOT duplicated; (2) R2F_FREDERICKIII_SICILY (Frederick III of Sicily) LIKEWISE ALREADY EXISTS in d3 — referenced as father of Peter II of Sicily, not duplicated (so Alfonso IV of Aragon and Peter II of Sicily are documented first cousins, both grandsons of Peter III the Great); (3) Blanche of Anjou (mother of Alfonso IV) and Eleanor of Anjou (mother of Peter II of Sicily) are BOTH added as daughters of the EXISTING R2G_CHARLES2_NAPLES + R2G_MARY_HUNGARY union (from 41_grandparents_d4.json) — i.e. Charles II of Naples is great-grandfather of both cousins; (4) Manuel of Castile (father of Juan Manuel of Villena) is added as a son of the EXISTING FERDIII + BEATSWAB union (Ferdinand III of Castile & Beatrice of Swabia, base tree); (5) Elizabeth of Rhuddlan (mother of William de Bohun) is added as a daughter of the EXISTING EI + EOC union (Edward I & Eleanor of Castile, base tree); (6) Philip III of Navarre (father of Charles II of Navarre) is added as a son of the EXISTING R2G_LOUIS_EVREUX (Louis, Count of Évreux, from d4) with a new mother node Margaret of Artois; (7) Louis I of Anjou and Philip the Bold of Burgundy are BOTH merged as sons of the EXISTING JOHNIIFR + BONNELUX union (John II of France & Bonne of Luxembourg, base tree); (8) the shared new node R3E_LOUIS4_HRE (Louis IV, Holy Roman Emperor) parents BOTH Stephen II of Bavaria (by 1st wife Beatrix of Świdnica) and Albert I of Bavaria (by 2nd wife Margaret II of Hainaut); (9) within-batch kin link: Guy IV de Châtillon, Count of Saint-Pol (father of Mahaut de Châtillon-Saint-Pol, the 1293 one / 3rd wife of Charles of Valois) also parents a new son Jean de Châtillon, who is in turn the father of the OTHER batch Mahaut de Châtillon (the 1335 Countess of Saint-Pol) — so the two Mahauts are documented aunt & niece. DISAMBIGUATION: R2H_JOHNIDUKELORRAINE's mother 'Marie of Blois' (R3E_MARIE_BLOIS_LORRAINE, d.1380, dau. of Guy I of Blois-Châtillon, 2nd wife of Rudolph of Lorraine) is a DIFFERENT woman from the batch's own R2H_MARIEDEBLOISCHATILLON (Marie of Blois-Châtillon, 1345-1404, Duchess of Anjou, dau. of Charles of Blois) — kept as separate nodes. Sourced to Wikipedia except where flagged; the des Baux/Orsini-Nola, Beaumont-au-Maine, and Castilian Guzmán lines are Wikipedia + specialist genealogy where noted (Beaumont father flagged moderate-confidence). 👑 reserved for regnant kings/queens/emperors. Verified 2026-07-17.
